thresh.description: Defines an app-specific or catch-all load-gap threshold using fields such as app and gapthresh. When the measured app iteration gap exceeds this multiplier, uLoadWatch can post a load warning.

appcast_height.description: Sets the height of the appcast display pane in uMACView.

infocast_height.description: Sets the height of the infocast display pane. Supported values are 30 through 90 in increments of 5.

realmcast_channel.description: Selects which realmcast channel uMACView displays on startup.

watch_cluster.description: Selects the initial appcast or realmcast cluster watched by uMACView.

appcast_color_scheme.description: Sets the color scheme used by appcast panes.

content_mode.description: Sets the startup infocast content mode shown by uMACView, either appcast or realmcast.

infocast_font_size.description: Sets the font size used in the infocast content panes.

nodes_font_size.description: Sets the font size used in the nodes pane.

procs_font_size.description: Sets the font size used in the processes pane.

realmcast_color_scheme.description: Sets the color scheme used by realmcast panes.

realmcast_show_source.description: Controls whether the Source column is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_show_community.description: Controls whether the Community column is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_show_subscriptions.description: Controls whether the Subscriptions block is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_show_masked.description: Controls whether masked or filtered realmcast variables are shown.

realmcast_wrap_content.description: Controls whether long realmcast value text wraps across multiple lines.

realmcast_trunc_content.description: Controls whether long realmcast value text is truncated in the value column.

realmcast_time_format_utc.description: Controls whether realmcast time values are displayed as UTC time instead of elapsed local time.

refresh_mode.description: Sets whether uMACView refreshes on events, streams continuously, or starts paused.

appcast_font_size.description: Sets the font size used in appcast output panes.

finish_val.description: Sets the value that must be observed on finish_var before uMayFinish allows completion.

max_db_uptime.description: Sets the MOOSDB uptime, in seconds, after which uMayFinish exits regardless of finish_var state. A negative value disables this timeout.

finish_var.description: Names the MOOS variable uMayFinish monitors to decide whether the mission may finish.

nowatch.description: Names process patterns that uProcessWatch should ignore even if they appear in DB_CLIENTS or the Antler-launched process list.

summary_wait.description: Sets the maximum time between PROC_WATCH_SUMMARY postings. A negative value posts summaries only when watched process status changes.

watch.description: Names a process that uProcessWatch should monitor and report as running or missing. Optional mappings may associate a watched process with a status variable.

allow_retractions.description: Controls whether uProcessWatch retracts run warnings when a missing process reappears. true always allows retraction, false disables it, and a numeric value limits retraction to that many seconds.

post_mapping.description: Maps one uProcessWatch posting variable to another output variable name.

watch_all.description: Controls whether uProcessWatch automatically monitors processes discovered from DB_CLIENTS and the Antler launch list.

pLogger.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

compressalogs.description: Compresses generated alog files after logging completes when compression support is available.

doubleprecision.description: Sets how many digits pLogger writes for double-valued entries in alog output.

log.description: Adds a MOOS variable to pLogger synchronous logging output.

logauxsrc.description: Includes MOOS auxiliary source information in asynchronous log entries when available.

utclogdirectories.description: Uses UTC time when naming pLogger log directories.

wildcardexclusionlog.description: Writes wildcard-rejected variables to a separate exclusion log instead of silently omitting them.

wildcardomitpattern.description: Adds a wildcard pattern for MOOS variables that pLogger should omit from wildcard logging.

synclog.description: Controls synchronous logging. The value may include a boolean enable flag and an optional period.

loggingdirectorysummaryfile.description: Names the summary file where pLogger records the last opened MOOS log directory.
loggingdirectorysummaryfile.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

markdatatype.description: Controls whether pLogger marks asynchronous log entries with each message data type.
markdatatype.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

markexternalcommunitymessages.description: Controls whether pLogger marks log entries originating from external MOOS communities.
markexternalcommunitymessages.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

file.description: Sets the base name used for pLogger log files.
file.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

filetimestamp.description: Controls whether pLogger appends timestamp information to generated log file names.
filetimestamp.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

path.description: Sets the directory where pLogger writes log files.
path.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_plogger.pdf and local MOOS pLogger source

marker_shape.description: Sets the shape used when rendering map markers.

show_markers.description: Controls whether map markers themselves are rendered.

show_mlabels.description: Controls whether map-marker labels are rendered.

show_mlables.description: Misspelled source-discovered alias for show_mlabels. Prefer show_mlabels in new mission files.

marker_color.description: Sets the fill color used for map markers posted by pMapMarkers.

marker_ecolor.description: Sets the edge color used for map markers posted by pMapMarkers.

marker_lcolor.description: Sets the label color used for map markers posted by pMapMarkers.

marker_size.description: Sets the rendered size of map markers posted by pMapMarkers.

opfile.description: Names the operation-area file used by pMapMarkers to place or constrain map marker output.

bin_min_val.description: Sets the minimum value, in meters, for obstacle encounter histogram bins. A negative value leaves binning disabled.

collision_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle-obstacle encounter is classified as a collision. Flag values may use distance and vehicle/obstacle macros from the app.

collision_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle minimum distance, in meters, below which an encounter is classified as a collision.

collision_range.description: Deprecated documentation alias for collision_dist; use collision_dist in mission files for the local source version.

encounter_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle comes within the configured encounter distance of an obstacle.

encounter_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle distance, in meters, at which obstacle encounters begin being tracked.

encounter_range.description: Deprecated documentation alias for encounter_dist; use encounter_dist in mission files for the local source version.

near_miss_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle-obstacle encounter is classified as a near miss.

near_miss_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle minimum distance, in meters, below which an encounter is classified as a near miss unless it is already a collision.

near_miss_range.description: Deprecated documentation alias for near_miss_dist; use near_miss_dist in mission files for the local source version.

bin_delta.description: Sets the width, in meters, of range histogram bins used for obstacle encounter statistics. A negative value disables binning.

sensor_config.description: Defines one simulated generic sensor, including its label and reporting behavior.

color_source_pts.description: Sets the display color for rendered generic-sensor source points.

min_reset_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, allowed between sensor reset operations.

min_sensor_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, allowed between sensor measurement operations.

scope_transparency.description: Sets the transparency used when rendering the generic sensor scope/coverage region.

show_source_pts.description: Controls whether configured source points are rendered for visualization.

source_point.description: Adds a named or positioned source point used by the generic sensor simulation.

truncated_output.description: Misspelled legacy spelling for truncated_output; controls whether long MOOS-scope and behavior-scope output is truncated.

behaviors_concise.description: Controls whether idle and completed behavior lists are shown compactly on one line rather than one behavior per line.

display_bhv_posts.description: Controls whether the behavior-posts section is shown in the uHelmScope report.

display_moos_scope.description: Controls whether the MOOS variable scope section is shown in the uHelmScope report.

display_virgins.description: Controls whether scoped MOOS variables that have not yet received mail are displayed.

paused.description: Controls whether uHelmScope starts in paused display mode.

var.description: Adds a MOOS variable to the MOOS-scope section. Multiple var lines may be supplied.

mission_hash_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where pMissionHash posts the full mission hash. Set this to off to disable full-hash posting.

mhash_short_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where pMissionHash posts the short mission hash. The local source initializes this to MHASH; setting it to off disables the short-hash post.

history.description: Sets how many recent node records uFldPathCheck keeps for each vehicle when estimating present speed and checking path progress.

history_length.description: Sets how many historical path points uFldPathCheck retains for path checking.

app_logging.description: Enables app-level AppCasting logging for the current MOOS app. This records diagnostic app output and does not change the app-specific mission behavior.

max_appcast_events.description: Limits how many appcast events the current MOOS app retains in its diagnostic report history.

collision_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event is classified as a collision. Flag values may use event macros such as $CPA, $V1, and $V2.

collision_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle closest-point-of-approach distance, in meters, at or below which an encounter is classified as a collision.

collision_dist.description: Source alias for the inter-vehicle collision threshold; the accepted documented mission parameter is collision_range.

condition.description: Adds a logic condition that must evaluate true before collision monitoring and event reporting are active.

encounter_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event enters the encounter range.

encounter_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle CPA distance, in meters, at or below which a pair is tracked as an encounter. The local source default is 20 meters.

encounter_dist.description: Source alias for the inter-vehicle encounter threshold; the accepted documented mission parameter is encounter_range.

encounter_rings.description: Controls whether pMarineViewer range rings are posted around vehicles at the configured encounter range.

ignore_group.description: Ignores encounters only when both vehicles are in one of the listed groups.

near_miss_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event is classified as a near miss.

near_miss_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle CPA distance, in meters, at or below which an encounter is classified as a near miss unless it is already a collision.

pulse_duration.description: Sets how long, in seconds, the VIEW_RANGE_PULSE remains visible for collision or near-miss events.

pulse_range.description: Sets the radius, in meters, of the visual range pulse posted for collision or near-miss events.

pulse_render.description: Controls whether collision and near-miss events produce VIEW_RANGE_PULSE visual markers.

post_closest_range.description: Controls whether the current closest observed inter-vehicle range is posted to UCD_CLOSEST_RANGE.

post_closest_range_ever.description: Controls whether the closest inter-vehicle range ever observed since startup/reset is posted to UCD_CLOSEST_RANGE_EVER.

reject_group.description: Completely disregards encounters involving any vehicle in one of the listed groups.

report_all_encounters.description: If true, UCD_REPORT is posted for clear encounters as well as near misses and collisions.

absolute_time_gap.description: Sets the absolute time gap threshold used when evaluating memory-watch sample timing.

ignore.description: Adds a process or memory entry name to ignore in uMemWatch reporting.

watch_only.description: Restricts uMemWatch output to the named process or memory entry.

rate_frame.description: Sets the frame rate used by uFldDelve for display or animation updates.

plot_var.description: Adds a MOOS variable to plot in uPlotViewer.

draw_save_statue.description: Source-discovered misspelling of draw_save_status. It controls whether the save-status polygon visualization is drawn while the behavior is recovering toward the save polygon.

breached_altitude_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the altitude limit set by this behavior.

breached_depth_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the depth limit set by this behavior.

breached_poly_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the convex polygon op area limit set by this behavior.

breached_time_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the time limit set by this behavior.

draw_halt_status.description: Controls whether the halt-region status polygon/marker is posted for visualization.

draw_save_status.description: Controls whether the save-region status polygon/marker is posted for visualization.

max_time.description: Sets the maximum mission time, in seconds, allowed before the operating-region behavior reports a time breach.

recover_spd.description: The preferred speed for the vehicle when it is out of the save polygon, attempting to maneuver back in. The speed should be high enough to enable a turn, but likely slower than normal in case an exit is inevitable for other reasons. The default value is 0.8 meters per second.

visual_hints.description: Overrides operating-region polygon rendering hints posted for display. Accepted keys include vertex_color, edge_color, label_color, edge_size, and vertex_size.

dynamic_region_var.description: Names the MOOS variable that supplies dynamic operating-region polygons.

halt_dist.description: Sets the distance from the operating-region boundary where halt behavior begins.

recover_speed.description: Sets the speed used while recovering from an operating-region violation.

save_dist.description: Sets the distance from the operating-region boundary where save behavior begins.

save_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the vehicle reaches the configured save boundary condition.

savex_flag.description: Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted when the vehicle reaches the configured save boundary condition.

p1.description: Sets one endpoint of the primary leg-run track line in local x,y coordinates.

p2.description: Sets the other endpoint of the primary leg-run track line in local x,y coordinates.

leg.description: Defines the primary leg-run track line by center point, angle, and length rather than by explicit p1 and p2 endpoints.

leg_len.description: Sets a new length for the primary leg-run track while preserving its center and angle. The behavior recalculates both endpoints from the new length.

leg_length.description: Source-level spelling for setting the length of the primary leg-run track while preserving its center and angle.

leg_len_mod.description: Adds a runtime adjustment to the current primary-leg length while preserving the leg center and angle. The resulting length is bounded by leg_len_min.

leg_length_mod.description: Source-level spelling for adding a runtime adjustment to the current primary-leg length.

leg_len_min.description: Sets the minimum allowed leg length when leg length is modified dynamically. The behavior enforces a lower safety bound on this value.

leg_ang.description: Sets a new angle for the primary leg-run track while preserving its center and length.

leg_ang_mod.description: Adds a runtime adjustment to the current primary-leg angle while preserving the leg center and length.

full_leg.description: Requires the vehicle to complete the full configured leg before transitioning into turn behavior.

init_leg_mode.description: Selects the initial leg/turn mode used when the behavior starts. Use this when startup should force a particular first leg direction or mode.

lr_mode.description: Runtime status value indicating the current leg-run phase, such as leg1, leg2, turn1, or turn2. This is normally reported by the behavior, not configured by users.

lr_mode_report.description: Runtime coordination report containing the current leg-run mode, vehicle name, and timestamp for multi-vehicle coordination.

lr_os_degs.description: Runtime coordination value giving ownship position in degrees on the reference circle used by multi-vehicle leg-run coordination.

shift_pt.description: Moves the primary leg so its center is at the given x,y point while preserving the original leg length and angle.

shift_point.description: Source-level spelling for shifting the primary leg so its center is at the given x,y point while preserving length and angle.

variable.description: Runtime coordination field used by BHV_LegRun status reporting. It is not a primary user-facing mission tuning parameter.

vx1.description: Source-level coordinate for the x value of the first leg-run vertex.

vx2.description: Source-level coordinate for the x value of the second leg-run vertex.

turn_bias.description: Sets the turn-shape bias for both vertices. Values are clipped to [-100,100]; negative values bias toward a Williamson-style turn aligned with the track line.

turn1_bias.description: Sets the turn-shape bias for vertex 1 only.

turn2_bias.description: Sets the turn-shape bias for vertex 2 only.

turn_bias_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to turn_bias for both vertices, clipped to [-100,100].

turn1_bias_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-1 turn bias, clipped to [-100,100].

turn2_bias_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-2 turn bias, clipped to [-100,100].

turn1_dir.description: Sets the turn direction at vertex 1: port, star/starboard, or toggle.

turn2_dir.description: Sets the turn direction at vertex 2: port, star/starboard, or toggle.

turn1_ext.description: Adds extra straight-line distance beyond vertex 1 before beginning the turn.

turn2_ext.description: Adds extra straight-line distance beyond vertex 2 before beginning the turn.

turn_ext.description: Adds extra straight-line distance beyond both vertices before beginning each turn.

turn1_ext_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-1 turn extension distance.

turn2_ext_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-2 turn extension distance.

turn_ext_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the turn extension distance at both vertices.

tur1_rad.description: Legacy misspelling for turn1_rad; sets the turn radius at vertex 1.

turn1_rad.description: Sets the turn radius, in meters, for the generated turn at vertex 1 of the leg-run pattern.

turn2_rad.description: Sets the turn radius, in meters, for the generated turn at vertex 2 of the leg-run pattern.

turn_rad.description: Sets the turn radius, in meters, for generated turns at both vertices of the leg-run pattern.

turn_rad_min.description: Sets the minimum allowed turn radius when runtime turn-radius modifiers are applied.

turn1_rad_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-1 turn radius, not below turn_rad_min.

turn2_rad_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to the vertex-2 turn radius, not below turn_rad_min.

turn_rad_mod.description: Runtime additive adjustment to both turn radii, not below turn_rad_min.

turn_pt_gap.description: Sets spacing, in meters, between generated points making up the turn arcs.

leg_spds.description: Sets a schedule of transit speeds, in m/s, for successive leg runs.

leg_spds_onturn.description: Sets the speed used while turning at the end of a leg.

leg_spds_repeat.description: Controls whether the leg speed schedule repeats after the final speed is used.

mid_leg_pct.description: Sets the percent along a leg where configured mid-leg flags are posted.

mid_turn_pct.description: Sets the percent through a turn where configured mid-turn flags are posted.

midpct.description: Source alias for the percent through a leg or turn where mid-event flags are posted.

warn_overshoot.description: Controls whether the behavior warns when ownship overshoots the current leg-run track.

capture_radius.description: Sets the range tolerance, in meters, for capturing the next leg-run vertex or turn point.

leg_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted on leg-run leg events.

leg_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when either leg-run leg ends.

leg1_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the first leg-run leg ends.

leg2_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the second leg-run leg ends.

mid_leg_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted after the configured mid_leg_pct point on a leg is crossed.

mid_turn_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted after the configured mid_turn_pct point through a turn is crossed.

slip_radius.description: Sets the wider capture radius used when the vehicle has passed or is opening range from a leg-run vertex.

speed.description: Sets the default transit speed, in meters per second, for straight leg-run segments.

wpt_status_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where leg-run waypoint/leg status is posted.

repeat.description: Sets how many times the leg-run waypoint engine repeats the two-leg track before completion. Use the waypoint-engine repeat syntax accepted by the behavior source.

turn_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when either turn at the end of a leg completes.

turn1_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the turn at vertex 1 completes.

turn2_endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the turn at vertex 2 completes.

visual_hints.description: Overrides rendering hints for leg-run track, turn, and waypoint visual artifacts.

lr_tcoord_spd.description: The speed currently the target speed for the behavior based on its relative position to other vehicles. Only relevant when turn coordination is enabled between multiple vehicles.

lr_now_spd.description: The speed currently the target speed for the behavior based on its relative position to other vehicles.

cycleflag.description: Posts a flag each time the behavior completes a waypoint/leg cycle.

leg1_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for leg1_endflag; posts a flag when the first leg-run leg ends.

leg2_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for leg2_endflag; posts a flag when the second leg-run leg ends.

leg_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for leg_endflag; posts a flag when either leg-run leg ends.

midflag.description: Source alias for a mid-leg or mid-turn event flag posted at the configured mid percentage.

midlegflag.description: Source alias for mid_leg_flag; posts when the configured mid-leg percentage is crossed.

midturnflag.description: Source alias for mid_turn_flag; posts when the configured mid-turn percentage is crossed.

radius.description: Compact spelling for capture_radius. It sets the range tolerance used when deciding that a leg-run waypoint has been captured.

turn1_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for turn1_endflag; posts when the turn at vertex 1 completes.

turn2_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for turn2_endflag; posts when the turn at vertex 2 completes.

turn_end_flag.description: Alias spelling for turn_endflag; posts when either leg-run turn completes.

wptflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a leg-run waypoint or vertex is reached.

lead.description: Sets the trackline lead distance used for leg-run path following. Larger values look farther ahead on the leg when producing course corrections.

fierce_zigging.description: Requests the zig leg with a direct relative-heading command rather than the gentler default zig behavior.

max_zig_legs.description: Sets how many zig legs must be completed before the behavior can complete.

max_zig_zags.description: Sets the maximum number of complete zig-zag cycles before the behavior can complete.

max_stem_odo.description: Sets the maximum distance traveled along the stem line before the behavior completes. A negative value disables this completion criterion.

zig_angle.description: Sets the relative angle used for the ordinary zig leg.

zig_angle_fierce.description: Sets the relative heading request used when fierce_zigging is enabled.

zig_first.description: Selects which side is used for the first zig. Supported values include port and starboard/star.

mod_speed.description: Apply a delta speed modification to the prevailing speed value. Typically applied during a mission rather than at mission startup.

speed.description: Speed in m/s to maintain during zig zag maneuvers. Default value is zero (must be set).

speed_on_active.description: The goal speed is set to the current ownship speed when the behavior becomes active. Default is false.

stem_on_active.description: Stem heading is set to ownship heading when the behavior becomes active. Default is false.

visual_hints.description: Configures viewer rendering for zig-zag artifacts, including requested-heading and set-heading colors.

draw_req_hdg.description: Controls whether the requested zig-zag heading is drawn for viewer feedback.

draw_set_hdg.description: Controls whether the actual commanded zig-zag set heading is drawn for viewer feedback.

max_stem_dist.description: Sets the maximum stem distance before the zig-zag pattern ends.

portflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the zig-zag behavior enters or completes a port-side leg.

req_hdg_color.description: Sets the color used to draw the requested zig-zag heading.

set_hdg_color.description: Sets the color used to draw the commanded zig-zag heading.

spd_on_active.description: Sets the speed command value posted when the zig-zag behavior becomes active.

starflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the zig-zag behavior enters or completes a starboard-side leg.

zagflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a zag leg event occurs.

zigflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when a zig leg event occurs.

portflagx.description: Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted when the zig-zag behavior enters or completes a port-side leg.

starflagx.description: Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted when the zig-zag behavior enters or completes a starboard-side leg.

center_assign.description: Moves the loiter polygon center to the given x,y point during configuration or updates.

xcenter_assign.description: Moves only the x-coordinate of the loiter polygon center, preserving the current y-coordinate.

ycenter_assign.description: Moves only the y-coordinate of the loiter polygon center, preserving the current x-coordinate.

post_suffix.description: Adds a suffix to MOOS variables posted by this loiter behavior so multiple loiter instances can be distinguished.

slingshot.description: Sets the bearing-change threshold used for loiter slingshot handling when approaching the polygon. A negative value leaves slingshot handling disabled.

acquire_dist.description: Distance to polygon outside which the behavior will be in ”acquire” mode.

capture_radius.description: The radius tolerance, in meters, for satisfying the arrival at a point.

polygon.description: Defines the loiter polygon to traverse. The vehicle follows the polygon vertices clockwise or counter-clockwise according to the behavior settings.

radius.description: Compact spelling for capture_radius. It sets the tolerance used to decide when the vehicle has reached a loiter vertex.

slip_radius.description: An ”outer” capture radius. Arrival declared when the vehicle is in this range and the distance to the point begins to increase.

speed.description: Sets the target transit speed, in meters per second, while moving around the loiter polygon.

use_alt_speed.description: If true then attempt to use the alternate speed set with the speed alt parameter, if that speed is set to a non-negative value. The default is false.

visual_hints.description: Overrides loiter visualization hints for the polygon and next-point marker. Accepted keys include vertex_size, edge_size, vertex_color, edge_color, nextpt_color, nextpt_lcolor, nextpt_vertex_size, label, lcolor, and label_color.

acquire_distance.description: Sets the distance to the loiter polygon at which the behavior considers the vehicle to have acquired the loiter pattern.

edge_color.description: Sets the color of the loiter polygon edges posted for viewer display.

edge_size.description: Sets the line width of the loiter polygon edges posted for viewer display.

label_color.description: Sets the label color of the loiter polygon posted for viewer display.

lcolor.description: Alias for the loiter polygon label color posted for viewer display.

nextpt_color.description: Sets the color used to draw the next waypoint point.

nextpt_lcolor.description: Sets the label color used for the next waypoint point.

nextpt_vertex_size.description: Sets the vertex size used for the next waypoint point.

nm_radius.description: Sets the nonmonotonic arrival radius. Within this radius, the waypoint engine may declare arrival/no-more progress even if range is no longer monotonically decreasing.

vertex_color.description: Sets the color of the loiter polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

vertex_size.description: Sets the size of the loiter polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

label.description: Sets the label applied to loiter visualization objects so the polygon and related viewer artifacts can be identified.

mod_poly_rad.description: Runtime update parameter that expands or contracts the loiter polygon radius relative to its current geometry.

compression.description: Compresses the ideal convoy range and speed policy so the follower can close distance more aggressively.

tail_length_max.description: Sets the maximum marker tail length retained for convoy visualization before older tail markers are dropped.

rng_estop.description: Alias-style source parameter for range_estop. It sets the close convoy range at which emergency stopping behavior is triggered.

rng_lagging.description: Alias-style source parameter for range_lagging. It sets the convoy range above which ownship is considered too far behind the lead/contact vehicle.

rng_tgating.description: Sets the convoy tailgating range where the follower is considered too close but not necessarily in emergency stop.

capture_radius.description: The range between the convoying vehicle and the next marker before the marker can be considered captured.

faster_convoy_range.description: The convoy range above which the behavior will produce preferred speeds that are faster than the lead vehicle speed.

full_stop_convoy_range.description: Range between ownship and the lead vehicle, within which the preferred ownship speed is zero.

full_lag_convoy_range.description: The convoy range above which ownship will apply maximum speed in order to close the convoy range. Maximum speed is given by the lead vehicle speed plus the lag speed delta.

inter_mark_range.description: Sets spacing, in meters, between generated convoy markers dropped along the lead vehicle track.

ideal_convoy_range.description: The ideal or target convoy range to be achieved by the behavior.

radius.description: Compact spelling for capture_radius. It sets the range within which ownship may capture the next convoy marker.

slip_radius.description: The range between the convoying vehicle and the next marker, within which the marker will be considered captured when or if the vehicle begins to open range to the marker.

slower_convoy_range.description: The convoy range within which the behavior will produce preferred speeds that are slower than the lead vehicle speed.

visual_hints.description: Controls the size and color of convoy marker visualizations posted for pMarineViewer.

cruise_speed.description: Sets the nominal speed used by the convoy behavior when it is neither speeding up to close range nor slowing for tailgating.

max_mark_range.description: Sets the farthest contact range at which convoy mark points are retained.

nm_radius.description: Sets the nonmonotonic arrival radius. Within this radius, the waypoint engine may declare arrival/no-more progress even if range is no longer monotonically decreasing.

rng_tailgating.description: Alias-style source parameter for range_tailgating. It sets the close convoy range where ownship is considered to be tailgating.

spd_max.description: Sets the maximum speed command the convoy behavior may request while trying to maintain convoy spacing.

peakwidth.description: Sets the width, in meters per second, of the peak region in the speed utility function.

summitdelta.description: Sets the summit-delta utility drop for the max-speed objective. It controls how sharply utility changes near the maximum permitted speed.
summitdelta.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

basewidth.description: Sets the speed band, in meters per second, over which utility tapers as ownship approaches the maximum speed constraint. Wider values make the max-speed constraint ramp in more gradually.
basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

speed.description: The desired speed of the vehicle, in meters per second.

speed_mismatch_var.description: Name of the MOOS variable indicating the present delta between the desired speed and the current speed. If left unspecified, no posting is made.

max_speed.description: Sets the maximum permitted vehicle speed enforced by BHV_MaxSpeed before speed slack or violation reporting occurs.

speed_slack_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where BHV_MaxSpeed posts remaining speed slack, computed as max_speed minus current NAV_SPEED.

period_busy.description: Sets the duration, in seconds, of the busy/high-speed portion of the periodic speed cycle.

period_lazy.description: Sets the duration, in seconds, of the lazy/low-speed portion of the periodic speed cycle.

basewidth.description: Sets the base width, in meters per second, for the documented periodic-speed ZAIC objective. It controls how quickly utility falls as speed differs from the desired periodic speed.
basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

peakwidth.description: Sets the peak width, in meters per second, for the documented periodic-speed ZAIC objective. A nonzero peak width gives a band of speeds maximum utility.
peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

period_speed.description: Sets the target speed, in meters per second, used during the busy/running phase of the periodic-speed objective.

summit_delta.description: Sets the summit-delta utility drop in the periodic-speed ZAIC objective. It shapes how strongly the behavior prefers the exact desired speed over nearby speeds.
summit_delta.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

period_basewidth.description: Sets the base width, in speed units, for the periodic-speed objective during the active speed pulse. It controls how far from the desired period speed utility tapers.
period_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

period_peakwidth.description: Sets the peak width, in speed units, for the periodic-speed objective during the active speed pulse. A nonzero value creates a flat high-utility band around the target speed.
period_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

zaic_basewidth.description: Deprecated spelling for basewidth; sets the base width, in speed units, for the underlying speed ZAIC used by BHV_PeriodicSpeed.
zaic_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

zaic_peakwidth.description: Sets the peak width, in speed units, for the underlying speed ZAIC used by BHV_PeriodicSpeed. A nonzero value creates a plateau of equally preferred speeds.
zaic_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

zaic_summit_delta.description: Sets the summit-delta utility drop for the speed ZAIC used by BHV_PeriodicSpeed. Larger values sharpen preference for the exact desired speed.
zaic_summit_delta.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

ascent_grade.description: Controls how desired speed is reduced during ascent toward the surface. The default linear grade approaches zero speed steadily.

period.description: Sets the duration, in seconds, of the waiting interval before the behavior next requests surfacing.

acomms_mark_variable.description: Configures acoustic-comms marking as variable,interval,max_time. New mail on the variable extends the surface-wait clock in interval-sized increments up to max_time.

ascent_speed.description: Desired speed of the vehicle during the ascent mode.

atsurface_status_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where the behavior posts elapsed seconds spent at the surface, resetting to zero when leaving the surface state.

mark_variable.description: Names the MOOS variable monitored for new mail that resets the periodic-surface idle clock. A changed numeric or string value marks the start of a new wait period.

pending_status_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where the behavior posts remaining seconds before the next required surfacing event, or ERROR when the timing state is invalid.

atsurface_status_variable.description: Alias for atsurface_status_var; names the MOOS variable where elapsed seconds at the surface are posted.

pending_status_variable.description: Alias for pending_status_var; names the MOOS variable where remaining seconds before the next required surfacing event are posted.

BHV_Trail.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP bhv_trail.pdf and IvP contact behavior source

trail_angle.description: Sets the relative angle behind or around the contact used to place the trail point.

n_alert_request.description: Suppresses or controls the automatic contact-manager alert request normally used by contact behaviors.

nm_radius.description: If in this range to contact and ahead of it, slow down. The default is 20 meters.

post_trail_dist_on_idle.description: If true, post TRAIL DISTANCE even during the idle state. The default is true.

pwt_outer_dist.description: Range to contact outside which the behavior has zero priority.

radius.description: If outside this radius to the contact, head to nm radius ahead of trail point. The default is 5 meters.

trail_range.description: Relative distance to the contact to set the trail-point. The default is 50 meters.

cncrs.description: Sets the contact course used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnlat.description: Sets the contact latitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnlon.description: Sets the contact longitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnspd.description: Sets the contact speed used by contact-geometry calculations.

mod_trail_range.description: Applies a runtime adjustment to the desired trail range.

oslat.description: Sets the ownship latitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

oslon.description: Sets the ownship longitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

efficiency_measure.description: Selects waypoint efficiency reporting. The local source accepts off to disable reporting and all to post leg and summary efficiency measurements.

order.description: Sets the order in which waypoints are traversed. Supported values include normal, reverse, and toggle.

greedy_tour.description: Allows waypoint ordering to be optimized greedily before traversal.

reset_on_idle.description: Resets waypoint progress when the behavior becomes idle.

shortest_tour.description: Requests shortest-tour ordering of the waypoint set before traversal.

capture_radius.description: The radius tolerance, in meters, for satisfying the arrival at a waypoint. The default is 3.

cycleflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted at the end of each completed pass through the waypoint list.

polygon.description: Alternative spelling for points. It accepts a colon-separated waypoint list and does not require the points to form a convex polygon.

post_suffix.description: A suffix tagged onto the WPT STATUS, WPT INDEX and CYCLE INDEX variables.

radius.description: Compact spelling for capture_radius. It sets the arrival radius used to decide when a waypoint has been reached.

repeat.description: Sets how many extra cycles through the waypoint list are performed, or forever for endless cycling. A positive repeat also makes the behavior perpetual in the local source.

slip_radius.description: An ”outer” capture radius. Arrival declared when the vehicle is in this range and the distance to the next waypoint begins to increase. The default is 15 meters.

speed.description: Sets the non-negative target transit speed, in meters per second, for moving through the waypoint list. The local source defaults this to zero until configured.

use_alt_speed.description: If true then attempt to use the alternate speed set with the speed alt parameter, if that speed is set to a non-negative value. The default is false.

visual_hints.description: Overrides waypoint visualization properties such as next-point color, path color, vertex size, and labels.

wpt_status_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where waypoint status messages are posted instead of the default status variable.

wpt_index_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where the current waypoint index is posted instead of the default index variable.

wptflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted after each waypoint is reached.

currix.description: Sets the current waypoint index used internally by the waypoint behavior when resuming or updating waypoint progress.

cycle_index_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to post the current waypoint-cycle index when the waypoint behavior repeats its route.

desired_speed.description: Sets the desired transit speed for this BHV_Waypoint behavior.

draw_path_loop.description: When true, renders the posted waypoint path as a closed loop connecting the final waypoint back to the first waypoint.

eager_prev_index_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior eagerly advances or records the previous waypoint index.

end_spd.description: Sets the speed used near the end of a waypoint sequence.

nm_radius.description: Sets the nonmonotonic arrival radius. Within this radius, the waypoint engine may declare arrival/no-more progress even if range is no longer monotonically decreasing.

osx.description: Sets the ownship x-coordinate used by the behavior's geometry and objective-function calculations, normally supplied from NAV_X/NAV_Y during runtime.

osy.description: Sets the ownship y-coordinate used by the behavior's geometry and objective-function calculations, normally supplied from NAV_X/NAV_Y during runtime.

ptx.description: Sets the target point x-coordinate used by the behavior's target-point geometry and objective-function calculations.

pty.description: Sets the target point y-coordinate used by the behavior's target-point geometry and objective-function calculations.

slow_dist.description: Sets the distance from the next waypoint where slowing begins.

stop_dist.description: Sets the distance from the next waypoint where stopping is requested.

point.description: Adds a single waypoint in local x,y coordinates. Multiple point lines may be used to build the waypoint sequence.

newpt.description: Runtime update parameter that adds or replaces a waypoint point in the active waypoint sequence.

lead.description: Sets the trackline lead distance used for waypoint path following. Larger values look farther ahead on the route when producing course corrections.

eval_tol.description: Sets the tolerance used when evaluating candidate COLREGS maneuvers.

passing_side.description: Selects which side the COLREGS passing maneuver is evaluated or preferred on.

post_status_info_on_idle.description: Keeps posting COLREGS status information even while the behavior is idle.

velocity_filter.description: Applies a velocity-based contact filter before evaluating COLREGS maneuvers.

completed_dist.description: Range to contact outside of which the behavior completes and dies.

max_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact outside which a considered maneuver will have max utility.

giveway_bow_dist.description: In the giveway consideration, when determining if ok to cross the contact’s bow, this range to contact at time of crossing from contact’s port to starboard, must be met in order for ownship to consider crossing the contact's bow. This is a new option in V19 of this behavior. If left unspecified, it will revert to the criteria used in previous versions.

min_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact within which a considered maneuver will have min utility.

pwt_inner_dist.description: Range to contact within which the behavior has maximum priority weight.

pwt_outer_dist.description: Range to contact outside which the behavior has zero priority weight.

all_clear_distance.description: Sets the contact range beyond which an avoidance maneuver is considered all clear.

collision_distance.description: Sets the contact or obstacle range treated as a collision.

osh.description: Sets the ownship heading supplied to geometry calculations.

osv.description: Sets the ownship speed supplied to geometry calculations.

pts_port_turns_ok.description: Controls whether point-turn style port turns are allowed during COLREGS maneuver generation.

turn_radius.description: Sets the assumed turning radius for maneuver feasibility checks.

plateau_region.description: Supplies the plateau-check polygon used when validating or refining the COLREGS avoidance objective function.

verbose.description: Enables detailed COLREGS behavior diagnostic output for debugging avoidance decisions.

avoid_mode.description: Selects or reports the current COLREGS encounter mode used by the avoidance logic, such as headon, overtaking, giveway, standon, cpa, or none.

avoid_submode.description: Selects or reports the current COLREGS submode within the active encounter mode, such as port, starboard, bow, stern, or inextremis depending on the mode.

full_avoid_mode.description: Reports the combined COLREGS mode and submode string used for status/debug output, combining avoid_mode with avoid_submode.

cnh.description: Sets the contact heading used by the avoidance geometry calculations.

cnv.description: Sets the contact speed used by the avoidance geometry calculations.

refine_piece.description: Enables objective-function piece refinement for the collision-avoidance utility surface.

roc_max_dampen.description: Limits how much rate-of-closure can dampen collision-avoidance utility.

roc_max_heighten.description: Limits how much rate-of-closure can heighten collision-avoidance utility.

completed_dist.description: Range to contact outside of which the behavior completes and dies. The default is 500 meters.

max_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact outside which a considered maneuver will have max utility. The default is 75 meters

min_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact within which a considered maneuver will have min utility. The default is 10 meters.

pwt_inner_dist.description: Range to contact within which the behavior has maximum priority weight. The default is 50 meters.

pwt_outer_dist.description: Range to contact outside which the behavior has zero priority weight. The default is 200 meters.

all_clear_distance.description: Sets the contact range beyond which an avoidance maneuver is considered all clear.

cnx.description: Sets the contact x-coordinate supplied to contact-geometry calculations.

cny.description: Sets the contact y-coordinate supplied to contact-geometry calculations.

collision_distance.description: Sets the contact or obstacle range treated as a collision.

osh.description: Sets the ownship heading supplied to geometry calculations.

osv.description: Sets the ownship speed supplied to geometry calculations.

osx.description: Sets the ownship x-coordinate used by the behavior's geometry and objective-function calculations, normally supplied from NAV_X/NAV_Y during runtime.

osy.description: Sets the ownship y-coordinate used by the behavior's geometry and objective-function calculations, normally supplied from NAV_X/NAV_Y during runtime.

plateau_region.description: Supplies the plateau-check polygon used when validating the collision-avoidance objective function.

refine_region.description: Supplies the polygonal region where the collision-avoidance objective function is refined into more detailed pieces.

verbose.description: Enables detailed collision-avoidance diagnostic output for debugging contact and objective-function decisions.

action.description: Selects the requested obstacle action, such as enabling, disabling, or otherwise changing a matching obstacle.

obid.description: Matches an obstacle by obstacle id for the requested obstacle action.

obstacle_key.description: Matches an obstacle by obstacle key for the requested obstacle action.

vsource.description: Matches an obstacle by visualization/source identifier for the requested obstacle action.

completed_dist.description: Range to contact outside of which the behavior completes and dies. The default is 500 meters.

max_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact outside which a considered maneuver will have max utility. The default is 75 meters

min_util_cpa_dist.description: Range to contact within which a considered maneuver will have min utility. The default is 10 meters.

pwt_inner_dist.description: Range to contact within which the behavior has maximum priority weight. The default is 50 meters.

pwt_outer_dist.description: Range to contact outside which the behavior has zero priority weight. The default is 200 meters.

polygon.description: Defines the convex obstacle polygon that this behavior will avoid. The polygon is normally supplied by obstacle-management mail or directly configured for a single known obstacle.

poly.description: Short spelling for the obstacle polygon parameter. It defines the convex obstacle polygon used by the avoidance behavior when a compact parameter name is preferred.

rng_flag.description: A flag to be posted on all iterations. It may be conditioned on a range threshold.

cpa_flag.description: A flag to be posted upon reaching the closest point of approach to the obstacle.

visual_hints.description: Overrides visual rendering hints for the obstacle polygon and its buffer polygons, such as colors, edge sizes, labels, and vertex sizes.

draw_buff_max_poly.description: Controls drawing of the larger buffered obstacle polygon used for visualizing the maximum avoidance buffer around the obstacle.

draw_buff_min_poly.description: Controls drawing of the smaller buffered obstacle polygon used for visualizing the minimum avoidance buffer around the obstacle.

radius.description: Sets the obstacle buffer radius used when expanding point-like obstacle geometry into an avoidance polygon.

turn_radius.description: Sets the assumed turning radius for maneuver feasibility checks.

basin_region.description: Supplies the basin region used by obstacle-avoidance processing to constrain candidate maneuver evaluation around the obstacle.

plateau_region.description: Supplies the plateau-check polygon used when validating or refining the generated obstacle-avoidance objective function.

max_window_utility.description: Sets the utility value assigned at the preferred side of the hysteresis window, shaping how strongly the behavior favors staying inside that window.

bearing_line*.description: Configures bearing-line visualization between ownship and a contact. The color thresholds map either relevance or range values to line colors, and related boolean parameters control whether the line and its label are shown.

match_*.description: Adds a contact filter that requires the contact to match the named field before the behavior treats it as a valid contact.

ignore_*.description: Adds a contact filter that excludes contacts matching the named field before the behavior treats them as valid contacts.

*_on_active.description: Controls or sets the value posted when the behavior becomes active.

nostarve.description: Requires one or more MOOS variables to stay fresh for the behavior to remain runnable. The value is a comma-separated list of variable names followed by the maximum allowed age in seconds.

no_starve.description: Alias of nostarve. It requires one or more MOOS variables to stay fresh for the behavior to remain runnable, with the final comma-separated value giving the allowed age in seconds.

bearing_lines.description: Configures contact bearing-line visualization as color:threshold pairs, optionally including info=range or info=relevance. When valid thresholds are provided, bearing-line drawing is enabled.

bearing_line_config.description: Configures contact bearing-line visualization as color:threshold pairs, optionally including info=range or info=relevance. This is the source-level spelling accepted by IvP contact behaviors.

bearing_line_show.description: Turns contact bearing-line drawing on or off when the behavior supports bearing-line visualization.

bearing_line_label_show.description: Controls whether labels are shown on contact bearing-line visualizations.

match_group.description: Requires the contact group to match the configured group before the behavior treats the contact as valid.

match_name.description: Requires the contact name to match the configured name before the behavior treats the contact as valid.

match_region.description: Requires the contact to be inside the configured convex region before the behavior treats the contact as valid.

match_type.description: Requires the contact type to match the configured type before the behavior treats the contact as valid.

ignore_group.description: Excludes contacts in the configured group before the behavior treats them as valid.

ignore_name.description: Excludes contacts with the configured name before the behavior treats them as valid.

ignore_region.description: Excludes contacts inside the configured convex region before the behavior treats them as valid.

ignore_type.description: Excludes contacts with the configured type before the behavior treats them as valid.

parameter.description: Documentation table placeholder, not an ordinary behavior configuration parameter. Prefer the specific OpRegion parameters such as core_poly, save_poly, halt_poly, max_depth, min_altitude, and reset.

core_poly.description: Defines the nominal operating area as a convex polygon. OpRegion behaviors use this as the primary region for containment and for deriving save/halt regions when distance offsets are configured.

save_poly.description: Defines the soft recovery polygon. When the vehicle exits this polygon, the behavior may produce an IvP function to guide it back inside before a harder halt breach occurs.

halt_poly.description: Defines the hard containment polygon. When the vehicle exits this polygon, the behavior signals a halt/all-stop style polygon breach.

max_depth.description: Sets the maximum allowable ownship depth in meters. A non-positive value leaves the depth limit disabled.

min_altitude.description: Sets the minimum allowable ownship altitude above the bottom in meters. A non-positive value leaves the altitude limit disabled.

reset.description: Resets a previously breached OpRegion condition back to its launch-like monitoring state. This is most useful when sent through the behavior updates variable during a mission.

activeflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior enters or remains in the active/running state.

active_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior enters or remains in the active/running state.

inactiveflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior is inactive.

inactive_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior is inactive.

idleflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior is idle because conditions or required information are not satisfied.

idle_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior is idle because conditions or required information are not satisfied.

endflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior completes.

end_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior completes.

runflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted while the behavior is running.

run_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted while the behavior is running.

runxflag.description: Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted while the behavior is running.

runx_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted while the behavior is running.

spawnflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted by the behavior spawning mechanism.

spawn_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted by the behavior spawning mechanism.

spawnxflag.description: Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted by the behavior spawning mechanism.

spawnx_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds an offboard variable-value flag posted by the behavior spawning mechanism.

configflag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior configuration is processed.

config_flag.description: Alias spelling. Adds a variable-value flag posted when the behavior configuration is processed.

condition.description: Adds a logic condition that must evaluate true before the behavior may run.

duration.description: Sets the maximum running duration, in seconds, before the behavior completes. Use -1 or no-time-limit for no duration limit.

duration_idle_decay.description: Controls whether the duration clock continues to decay while the behavior is idle.

duration_reset.description: Names a MOOS variable-value trigger that resets the behavior duration clock when new matching mail arrives.

duration_status.description: Names the MOOS variable where the behavior posts remaining duration or duration status updates.

name.description: Sets the behavior descriptor/name used in status reports, postings, and update targeting.

descriptor.description: Source-level descriptor for the behavior instance, used in status reports, posting keys, and update targeting. In mission files this is normally set with name.

perpetual.description: If true, the behavior resets after completion and may run again instead of completing permanently.

post_mapping.description: Remaps behavior output from one MOOS variable to another. Mapping to silent suppresses that posting.

priority.description: Sets the behavior priority weight used by the helm when combining objective functions.

pwt.description: Alternative spelling of priority. It sets the priority weight used by the helm when combining this behavior with other objective functions.

priwt.description: Alternative spelling of priority. It sets the priority weight used by the helm when combining this behavior with other objective functions.

updates.description: Names the MOOS variable from which this behavior accepts runtime update strings.

templating.description: Marks the behavior block as a template used for later spawning rather than a normal behavior instance.

build_info.description: Adds or clears behavior build information included in behavior status output.

visual_hints.description: Configures behavior-specific rendering hints such as colors, sizes, labels, or display toggles for viewer artifacts produced by the behavior.

strict_ignore.description: Controls whether ignore filters are applied strictly before contact matching succeeds.

exit_on_filter_group.description: If true, the contact behavior completes or exits when the contact fails the group filter.

exit_on_filter_region.description: If true, the contact behavior completes or exits when the contact fails the region filter.

exit_on_filter_vtype.description: If true, the contact behavior completes or exits when the contact fails the vehicle-type filter.

exit_on_filter_vname.description: If true, the contact behavior completes or exits when the contact fails the vehicle-name filter.

decay.description: Sets contact extrapolation decay as start and end times in seconds; extrapolated contact speed decays over that interval.

decay_end.description: Deprecated source-level parameter setting the end time, in seconds, for contact extrapolation decay.

on_no_contact_ok.description: Controls whether the behavior is allowed to run when no valid contact is currently available.

time_on_leg.description: Sets the elapsed time, in seconds, assumed for contact projection along its current leg.

heading_mismatch_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where BHV_ConstantHeading posts the current heading error between the desired heading and ownship heading.

basewidth.description: Sets the speed-error span, in meters per second, over which the speed ZAIC objective tapers away from the target speed. Wider values make nearby speeds more acceptable.
basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

peakwidth.description: Sets the speed-error span, in meters per second, around the target speed that receives peak utility. A nonzero peak width creates a flat preferred speed band.
peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

speed.description: Sets the target speed, in meters per second, that BHV_ConstantSpeed tries to maintain.

summitdelta.description: Sets the summit-delta utility drop for the speed ZAIC objective. Larger values sharpen preference for the exact desired speed.
summitdelta.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

speed_mismatch_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where BHV_ConstantSpeed posts the current speed error, computed as desired speed minus current NAV_SPEED.

giveup_dist.description: Range to contact, in meters, outside which the behavior will give up (become inactive). Default is zero.

giveupflag.description: Optional MOOS variable-value pairs posted when ownship range to contact increases above giveup dist.

pursueflag.description: Optional MOOS variable-value pairs posted when ownship range to contact drops below giveup dist.

pwt_outer_dist.description: Range to contact outside which the behavior has maximum priority.

pwt_inner_dist.description: Range to contact within which the behavior has zero priority.

cncrs.description: Sets the contact course used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnlat.description: Sets the contact latitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnlon.description: Sets the contact longitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

cnspd.description: Sets the contact speed used by contact-geometry calculations.

giveup_range.description: Sets the contact range at which the behavior gives up pursuing the target geometry.

oslat.description: Sets the ownship latitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

oslon.description: Sets the ownship longitude used by contact-geometry calculations.

speed.description: The default speed for any turn that does not specify a turn speed. The default value is 1 meter per second.

visual_hints.description: Overrides viewer rendering hints for the fixed-turn preview geometry, such as vertex size, edge color, vertex color, and label display.

radius_rep_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to report the fixed-turn radius.

capture_flag.description: The name of a MOOS variable incremented each time a target depth level has been achieved.

repeat.description: Sets how many additional times the configured depth sequence is repeated after the first traversal. The local source accepts non-negative integers.

arrival_flag.description: Posts a flag when BHV_GoToDepth reaches its target depth.

depth.description: Defines the target depth sequence. Each entry is depth or depth,time, with colon-separated entries used for multi-level depth plans.

basewidth.description: Sets the depth band, in meters, over which utility tapers as ownship approaches the maximum depth constraint. Wider values make the max-depth constraint ramp in more gradually.
basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

depth_slack_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where BHV_MaxDepth posts remaining depth slack, computed as max_depth minus current NAV_DEPTH.

tolerance.description: Alternative way to set the max-depth basewidth tolerance. It controls how close to the maximum depth ownship may get before utility strongly penalizes deeper commands.
tolerance.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

turn_range.description: The range of heading values deviating from the current heading average outside of which the behavior reflects sharp penalty in its objective function. The default value is -1, indicating that the parameter is un-set. In this case the behavior will not produce an objective function.

breached_altitude_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the altitude limit set by this behavior.

breached_depth_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the depth limit set by this behavior.

breached_poly_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the convex polygon op area limit set by this behavior.

breached_time_flag.description: A MOOS variable-value pair to be posted when or if the vehicle breaches the time limit set by this behavior.

draw_halt_status.description: Controls whether halt-region recovery status is posted as a visual artifact.

draw_save_status.description: Controls whether save-region recovery status is posted as a visual artifact.

max_time.description: Sets the maximum time, in seconds, allowed for recovery behavior before a time-breach condition is reported.

recover_spd.description: The preferred speed for the vehicle when it is out of the save polygon, attempting to maneuver back in. The speed should be high enough to enable a turn, but likely slower than normal in case an exit is inevitable for other reasons. The default value is 0.8 meters per second.

visual_hints.description: Overrides recovery-region polygon rendering hints posted for display. Accepted keys include vertex_color, edge_color, label_color, edge_size, and vertex_size.

buffer_dist.description: Sets the buffer distance around the operating region.

dynamic_region_var.description: Names the MOOS variable that supplies dynamic operating-region polygons.

edge_color.description: Sets the color of the recovery-region polygon edges posted for viewer display.

edge_size.description: Sets the line width of the recovery-region polygon edges posted for viewer display.

label_color.description: Sets the label color of the recovery-region polygon posted for viewer display.

opregion_poly_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to publish the operating-region polygon.

reset_var.description: Names the MOOS variable that resets recovery state.

vertex_color.description: Sets the color of the recovery-region polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

vertex_size.description: Sets the size of the recovery-region polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

heading_peakwidth.description: Sets the peak width, in degrees, of the heading objective used while shadowing the contact. A wider peak makes nearby headings equally preferred.
heading_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

heading_basewidth.description: Sets the base width, in degrees, of the heading objective used while shadowing the contact. Wider values allow a broader range of headings to retain utility.
heading_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

speed_peakwidth.description: Sets the peak width, in meters per second, of the speed objective used while shadowing the contact. A nonzero peak allows a range of speeds to share maximum utility.
speed_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

speed_basewidth.description: Sets the base width, in meters per second, of the speed objective used while shadowing the contact. It controls how quickly utility falls away from the target shadowing speed.
speed_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

hdg_basewidth.description: Source spelling for heading_basewidth. It sets the base width, in degrees, of the shadowing heading objective.
hdg_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

hdg_peakwidth.description: Source spelling for heading_peakwidth. It sets the peak width, in degrees, of the shadowing heading objective.
hdg_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

pwt_outer_dist.description: Sets the contact range threshold for Shadow relevance. At ranges below this distance the behavior has full relevance; at or beyond it the source returns zero relevance. A value of 0 keeps relevance fully on.

spd_basewidth.description: Source spelling for speed_basewidth. It sets the base width, in meters per second, of the shadowing speed objective.
spd_basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

spd_peakwidth.description: Source spelling for speed_peakwidth. It sets the peak width, in meters per second, of the shadowing speed objective.
spd_peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

hibernation_radius.description: Sets the passive station-keeping radius used for low-power hibernation behavior around the station point.

inner_radius.description: Sets the distance from the station point within which the target speed is zero and the behavior considers ownship close enough to hold station.

outer_radius.description: Sets the distance from the station point where the return-to-station speed reaches outer_speed; inside this radius speed ramps down toward zero at inner_radius.

outer_speed.description: Sets the target speed at the outer station-keeping radius. Between inner_radius and outer_radius, target speed ramps from zero up to this value.

transit_speed.description: Alias for extra_speed; sets the target transit speed used when ownship is beyond the outer station-keeping radius and returning toward the station point.

visual_hints.description: Overrides station-keeping region polygon rendering hints posted for display. Accepted keys include vertex_color, edge_color, label_color, edge_size, and vertex_size.

edge_color.description: Sets the color of the station-keeping region polygon edges posted for viewer display.

edge_size.description: Sets the line width of the station-keeping region polygon edges posted for viewer display.

extra_speed.description: Sets the extra speed used when returning to a station or contact-relative position.

label_color.description: Sets the label color of the station-keeping region polygon posted for viewer display.

vertex_color.description: Sets the color of the station-keeping region polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

vertex_size.description: Sets the size of the station-keeping region polygon vertices posted for viewer display.

nm_radius.description: Sets the nonmonotonic arrival radius. Within this radius, the waypoint engine may declare arrival/no-more progress even if range is no longer monotonically decreasing.

radius.description: Sets the arrival radius, in meters, used to decide when the abort-to-point destination has been reached.

mark_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when BHV_FullStop marks the vehicle as stopped.

unmark_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when BHV_FullStop clears or unmarks the stopped state.

turn_speed.description: Sets the speed used during the heading-change turn.

filter_var.description: Names the MOOS variable providing values for the hysteresis filter.

basewidth.description: Sets the heading-error span, in degrees, over which the ZAIC objective tapers from its preferred heading toward low utility. Wider base widths make the behavior tolerate a broader range of headings.
basewidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

peakwidth.description: Sets the heading-error span, in degrees, around the target heading that receives peak utility. A wider peak creates a flatter preferred heading region.
peakwidth.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

summitdelta.description: Sets the utility drop across the ZAIC summit for the heading objective. Larger values make the peak less flat and strengthen preference for the exact target heading.
summitdelta.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP behavior documentation/source review for objective-function shaping parameters

edge_color.description: Sets the edge color for the platform-model view polygon or outline.

vertex_color.description: Sets the vertex color for points in the platform-model view geometry.

vertex_size.description: Sets the rendered vertex size for the platform-model view geometry.

extra_speed.description: Sets the extra speed used when returning to a station or contact-relative position.

rstation_position.description: Specifies the desired relative station-keeping position with respect to the contact or reference point.

max_time.description: Sets the timeout duration, in seconds, after which BHV_TimeOut completes or posts its configured timeout effects.

var_status_idle.description: Names the MOOS variable posted with timer-idle status. The configured status suffix is appended when status is posted.

var_status_running.description: Names the MOOS variable posted with timer-running status. The configured status suffix is appended when status is posted.
